seo.title: Services and Capabilities
seo.description: Engineering, integration, and support services that surround the technical-product-showcase demo product lines.

sections.title: Engineering and integration services for serious technical buyers.
sections.description: Pre-sales engineering, integration, factory acceptance, deployment, and warranty support — wrapped around the same modular product platform.
sections.imageAlt: Engineering team reviewing schematics next to staged hardware

sections.title: Capability map
sections.description: Each capability is delivered against a written scope document with named owners and clear acceptance criteria.

items.title: Application engineering
items.description: Translate process and load requirements into a hardware specification with traceable design choices.

items.title: Mechanical integration
items.description: Cabinet design, mounting hardware, thermal management, and on-site fitment review.

items.title: Control software
items.description: PLC and HMI logic against IEC 61131-3, with a clean interface to the customer's SCADA or MES layer.

items.title: Machine vision
items.description: Lighting, optics, camera, and inference module selection sized to the customer's defect taxonomy and cycle time.

items.title: Energy and power conversion
items.description: Battery sizing, inverter and transformer selection, and grid-tie commissioning under local interconnection rules.

items.title: Factory acceptance
items.description: Documented acceptance test runs at the factory with the customer team present before equipment ships.

items.title: Site deployment
items.description: Logistics, installation supervision, on-site commissioning, and operator training within a fixed deployment window.

items.title: Diagnostics and warranty
items.description: Remote diagnostics access for the warranty term, with a written escalation path and target response times.

items.title: Spare parts program
items.description: Recommended spares list shipped with every system, with restocking lead times and serial-level traceability.

sections.title: Smart equipment integration
sections.description: Sensorise existing production lines without ripping out working hardware. The smart equipment program adds telemetry, condition monitoring, and remote diagnostics on top of installed assets, with clean ingestion into the customer's existing data layer.
sections.imageAlt: Engineer connecting a sensor module to an existing production line

sections.title: Energy systems engineering
sections.description: Battery storage and power conversion sized to the customer's tariff, load profile, and reliability targets. Each project ships with a written commissioning plan and a one-line diagram that interconnection authorities will recognise.
sections.imageAlt: Battery cabinets staged in a commercial equipment bay with conduit runs

steps.title: Discovery
steps.description: Walk the line, log constraints, agree the success metric, write it down.

steps.title: Specification
steps.description: Translate discovery output into a hardware and integration spec with acceptance criteria.

steps.title: Build and integrate
steps.description: Manufacture, integrate firmware, and bench-test against the spec at the factory.

steps.title: Deploy and operate
steps.description: Install, commission, train operators, and stay engaged through the warranty period.

items.title: Vision retrofit on a high-mix electronics line
items.description: Added an inline vision module and rework triage logic to an existing SMT line, lifting first-pass yield by four points without taking the line down for more than one shift.

items.title: Behind-the-meter storage for a regional distribution centre
items.description: Sized and commissioned a one-megawatt-hour storage system that smooths peak demand and provides four hours of cold-chain backup against grid events.

items.question: Do you take on integration work without selling hardware?
items.answer: Yes, when the existing equipment is in good condition and accessible. Integration-only engagements still ship with a written scope and acceptance criteria.

items.question: What is the warranty term?
items.answer: Twelve months from factory acceptance is standard. Extended terms are available on documented spare parts coverage and remote diagnostics access.

items.question: Can you work with our existing PLC vendor?
items.answer: In most cases, yes. The control software practice routinely interoperates with mainstream PLC and HMI vendors; less common stacks are reviewed case by case.
